definition:
1. to build a wall , (Aristophanes Comicus), etc.: with accusative cognate, τ. τεῖχος to build  it, (Thucydides); so in Mid., τεῖχος ἐτειχίσσαντο they built them  a wall, (Iliad by Homer):—;Pass. to be built , (Pindar): 3rd singular pluperfect τετείχιστο, impersonal, buildings had been erected, there were buildings , (Herdotus Historicus) 
2. transitive to wall  or fortify , τὸ οὖρος (Herdotus Historicus); τὴν πόλιν (Thucydides): in Mid., τειχίζεσθαι τὸ χωρίον (Thucydides):—;Pass. to be walled  or fenced with walls , (Thucydides); τὰ τετειχισμένα the fortified parts , (Thucydides) (ML)
